topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is a collection of various materials and tools used by System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: Who is the Book of Secret Knowledge repository aimed at?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is aimed at System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers, but it can be useful for everyone.

entries.q: What type of license does the Book of Secret Knowledge repository use?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository uses the MIT License.

entries.q: How can users contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository by making pull requests and following the contributing guidelines.

entries.q: What is the RSS/Atom feed used for in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The RSS/Atom feed is used to keep users informed about all changes and updates to the repository.

entries.q: What are some of the main chapters in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Some of the main chapters include CLI Tools, GUI Tools, Web Tools, Systems/Services, Networks, Containers/Orchestration, and more.

entries.q: What are some examples of CLI Tools mentioned in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Some examples of CLI Tools mentioned include GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, and Oh My ZSH!

entries.q: What are some examples of shell plugins mentioned in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Some examples of shell plugins mentioned include z, fzf, zsh-autosuggestions, and zsh-syntax-highlighting.
